patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the code attempting to parse the imaginary character from a substring which may exceed the length of the source string. This happens because the method doesn't ensure enough characters after the imaginary number to accommodate retrieving the imaginary character.
patches.changes: - Added a length check before attempting to parse the imaginary character to prevent StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.
patches.diff: - Added a length check before attempting to parse the imaginary character to prevent StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.

patches.hypothesis: The error is due to a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when the substring goes beyond the source length. The imaginary character check should be done only if there's enough string remaining.
patches.changes: - Added a check to ensure endIndex does not exceed the source length before substring comparison.
patches.diff: - Added a check to ensure endIndex does not exceed the source length before substring comparison.

patches.hypothesis: The method fails because it tries to access a substring without checking if the source string is long enough, leading to a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.
patches.changes: - Added check to ensure substring endIndex does not exceed source length.
patches.diff: - Added check to ensure substring endIndex does not exceed source length.

patches.hypothesis: The exception is caused by attempting to parse a substring beyond the end of the input string when the imaginary character is forgotten. The method should check if sufficient characters are remaining before attempting to parse the imaginary part.
patches.changes: + Handle insufficient length before substring check for imaginary char
patches.diff: + Handle insufficient length before substring check for imaginary char

patches.hypothesis: The code is attempting to extract a substring for the imaginary character without checking if there are enough characters in the source string, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException.
patches.changes: - Changed the code to check if there are enough characters left in the source string before attempting to parse the imaginary character.
patches.diff: - Changed the code to check if there are enough characters left in the source string before attempting to parse the imaginary character.

patches.hypothesis: The StringIndexOutOfBoundsException occurs because the code attempts to access a substring beyond the available length when the imaginary part is missing. We need to ensure the source length is sufficient before attempting the substring operation to avoid the exception.
patches.changes: - Added check to ensure there are enough characters in the source string before attempting to access the imaginary character.
patches.diff: - Added check to ensure there are enough characters in the source string before attempting to access the imaginary character.

patches.hypothesis: The error occurs due to an attempt to extract a substring past the end of the source string when the imaginary character is missing. The fix involves checking if the remaining characters are sufficient before attempting substring extraction.
patches.changes: Ensure sufficient string length before accessing substring
patches.diff: Ensure sufficient string length before accessing substring
